questionStem: A court mandates that an individual convicted of domestic violence attend therapy as a condition of probation. During the course of treatment, the prosecution subpoenas the therapist's records. In this court-ordered treatment scenario, who holds the therapist-client privilege?
choices: The client retains privilege and can prevent disclosure of therapy records
The therapist holds privilege and decides whether to disclose records
Privilege is typically waived or significantly limited because treatment was court-ordered
The client's defense attorney holds privilege on behalf of the client
